commit.message:
Skip password fields and pre-fill data for logged-in clients on purchase form

When a client is already logged in and navigates to buy a house,
the form now detects the active session, pre-fills their info from
the database, makes the email read-only, and hides the password
fields entirely since they already have an account.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign article typography: Pretendard font + full readability overhaul

- Add Pretendard Variable font (Korean-optimized)
- word-break: keep-all globally
- Article: font-size 1.05rem, line-height 2.0, letter-spacing Korean-optimized
- H2: left-border accent + subtle background highlight
- H3: left border accent
- Blockquotes: callout box style
- Tables: full redesign with hover, rounded corners, green header
- lab-card / lab-formula-box / lab-graph-wrapper: polished styles
- Post list cards: hover lift + shadow
- Mobile table responsiveness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Per-draw snapshots: standalone CSV + metadata after every draw

Each checkpoint now also produces a clean, deduplicated final CSV and
metadata JSON. The metadata records:
- n_bootstrap_completed: actual draws finished so far
- n_bootstrap: total requested draws
- complete: true only when all draws are done

This means results are immediately usable if a job is killed early.
The dispatcher checks "complete": true in metadata before marking a
job as done, so partial snapshots don't prevent re-queuing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add LCNA-INC-2026-008 validation report: luckycommon OpenSearch RED event

Validates original 2026-03-08 incident report against CloudWatch metrics.
Verdict: Scenario C — RED event isolated, but original report omitted 4
additional YELLOW node drop events spanning 162 minutes across 03/07–03/09.

Key findings:
- "Single isolated RED event" → PARTIALLY TRUE (RED isolated; node instability not)
- "No prior ES alerts in 7 days" → FALSE (Events 1 & 2 within 29 hours prior)
- "Node drop 7→6 for 1 minute" → CONFIRMED for Event 3
- "~41 GB ISM deletion" → SUPPORTED by doc count evidence
- "Search latency 8.1ms" → CONFIRMED (measured 8.13ms)

Actions: R1 IAM grant for databasecheck, R2 ISM audit, R3 report addendum,
R4 stagger ISM deletions with 4h+ gap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add bilingual support (Polish/English) with client-side locale detection

for UI components and getT() for async callbacks. Covers all user-visible strings
across dashboard, journal, gate, notifications, and voice notes.

fix: remove set -u flag for bash 3.2 compatibility

to use string accumulation instead of arrays.

commit.message:
feat: AES-256-GCM 클라이언트 암호화 — 비밀번호 없이 접근 불가

- index.html, council.html 전체 AES-256-GCM 암호화
- PBKDF2 100K iterations 키 도출
- EVA 테마 잠금 화면 (ACCESS CODE 입력)
- 틀리면 ACCESS DENIED + shake 애니메이션
- 맞으면 DECRYPTING → 원본 페이지 복호화 렌더링
- 원본(_src/)과 빌드툴은 .gitignore로 제외
- 소스 코드를 봐도 암호문만 보임

fix: ensure recordings directory exists before writing files

On fresh clones (e.g. Windows), the recordings/ directory doesn't exist,
causing writeFile to throw ENOENT. Use mkdir with recursive flag to
create it on demand.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: hardcode new Supabase URL to bypass Lovable env override

Lovable injects its own VITE_SUPABASE_URL at build time, overriding .env.
Hardcoding the URL ensures the frontend connects to our self-hosted project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: disable declaration-property-value-no-unknown stylelint rule

The @csstools/css-syntax-patches-for-csstree 1.1.0 update introduces
a <symbols()> syntax reference that breaks css-tree's lexer, causing
stylelint's declaration-property-value-no-unknown rule to crash.
Disable the rule until upstream compatibility is resolved.

commit.message:
feat(state): continuous state externalization to survive context compaction (v3.10.4)

New lib/state.sh writes ORCHESTRATOR_STATE.md at key lifecycle moments
(agent launch, status poll, merge) so reinject-context.sh has a fresh
snapshot when Claude Code compacts the session. Reduces the gap between
last known state and compaction to ~30s instead of losing all in-flight
reasoning.

commit.message:
docs: SF-DCC-001 session closeout + PK.md update [v3.3.0]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Kvm tuning guide (#3196)

* Add KVM/libvirt performance tuning guide for Rocky Linux

Covers NUMA topology, vm.min_free_kbytes dangers, vCPU pinning, CPU isolation,
tuned profiles, GPU passthrough with IOMMU/VFIO, slow boot diagnosis, and
hugepage configuration. Tested on Rocky Linux 8.10, 9.7, and 10.1.

Refs: CUSP-2078

Assisted-by: Claude claude-sonnet-4-6
Co-Authored-By: Howard Van Der Wal <hvanderwal@ciq.com>

* Update tested_with to use major versions only

Assisted-by: Claude claude-sonnet-4-6
Co-Authored-By: Howard Van Der Wal <hvanderwal@ciq.com>

* Move ai_contributors under tested_with in front matter

Assisted-by: Claude claude-sonnet-4-6
Co-Authored-By: Howard Van Der Wal <hvanderwal@ciq.com>

* Sort tags alphabetically in front matter

Assisted-by: Claude claude-sonnet-4-6
Co-Authored-By: Howard Van Der Wal <hvanderwal@ciq.com>

* Add full stops to topic list in introduction

Assisted-by: Claude claude-sonnet-4-6
Co-Authored-By: Howard Van Der Wal <hvanderwal@ciq.com>

* Add references, fix fact-verification issues, remove bold formatting

- Add inline superscript references and References section with upstream
  kernel.org and libvirt.org sources (no Red Hat references)
- Fix vfio_virqfd merge version (upstream kernel 6.2, not 5.14)
- Fix rcu_nocbs description (kthreads, not directly to housekeeping CPUs)
- Fix numatune strict mode behavior (OOM killer, not clean startup failure)
- Fix vm.min_free_kbytes terminology (lowmem zones, not all memory zones)
- Remove specific kernel version 5.14.0-558.el9 (unverifiable)
- Remove bold formatting from pinning rules list

Assisted-by: Claude claude-sonnet-4-6
Co-Authored-By: Howard Van Der Wal <hvanderwal@ciq.com>

* Fix vfio_virqfd note based on hardware testing

Testing on 192.168.1.102 with NVIDIA RTX A2000 confirmed:
- Rocky 8.10 (kernel 4.18): vfio_virqfd is a separate module
- Rocky 9.7 (kernel 5.14): vfio_virqfd merged into vfio (RHEL backport)
- Rocky 10.1 (kernel 6.12): vfio_virqfd merged into vfio

Also tested on hardware: IOMMU group listing, GPU hostdev XML,
X-PciMmio64Mb syntax, modprobe/dracut configs, and complete
domain XML definition with libvirt.

Assisted-by: Claude claude-sonnet-4-6
Co-Authored-By: Howard Van Der Wal <hvanderwal@ciq.com>

* Remove unverified GPU passthrough sections

Remove the large GPU BAR/OVMF configuration, the diagnosing slow VM boot
times section, and related references that could not be verified through
testing.

Assisted-by: Claude claude-opus-4-6
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove entire GPU passthrough section — not verified end-to-end

GPU passthrough could not be fully tested across Rocky Linux 8, 9, and 10.
Remove the section entirely along with related tags, prerequisites,
introduction references, conclusion bullet, and VFIO reference.
Update title to reflect remaining content.

Assisted-by: Claude claude-opus-4-6
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update prerequisites: relative doc link, add Rocky 8 and 10

Use relative path for libvirt-rocky.md link per Rocky Linux contribution
guidelines. Remove 64-bit machine requirement and add Rocky Linux 8 and 10.

Assisted-by: Claude claude-opus-4-6
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Change document title to KVM tuning

Assisted-by: Claude claude-opus-4-6

commit.message:
Add v2 request flow diagram with availability probe architecture

New architecture features:
- Redis-based channel availability filtering (group:model:channel:availability)
- Async recovery queue with exponential backoff retry
- Admin panel for manual channel health management

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add timeouts, error reporting, and scroll fix for processing pipeline

- Add fetch timeouts to all API calls: YouTube (15s), HubSpot (10s),
  Gemini content review (10min), Gemini channel resolver (5min)
- Add per-creator 12-minute timeout wrapper so batch never hangs
- Add classifyError() for user-friendly error messages (timeout, API
  failure, network error) instead of generic "Error"
- Surface error details in processing UI (e.g. "Timed out during:
  Running AI content review…")
- Add server-side timing logs for debugging in Vercel
- Fix auto-scroll bug: only scroll when a new creator starts processing,
  not on every step update
- Show step-level status messages during analysis (Resolving channel,
  Checking HubSpot, Pulling videos, etc.)
- Bump Vercel maxDuration to 15min for long rosters

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(design-system): parity checks — all 9 components scored

Typography components (Overline/FieldLabel/CardTitle/MutedText/StatValue): 94/100
BodyText: 91/100 — fixed Figma line-height to 26px (leading-relaxed, 1.625×16)
Divider: 86/100 — both flags false positives (float precision + fixed canvas width)
Button: 88/100, Card: 95/100 (scored in previous commit)

All discrepancies are either float-precision artifacts or React↔Figma paradigm
differences (children/className props). No code changes required.

chore: add open-source branching strategy and Docker deployment

- GitHub Actions CI: build + test + Docker build/push on every PR and main push
- Docker images published to GHCR (engine + dashboard) on merge to main
- Multi-stage Dockerfiles for Engine (port 8080) and Dashboard (port 8081)
- docker-compose.yml for local and self-hosted deployment
- .dockerignore to keep images lean
- .github/CODEOWNERS: @shyfaruqi required on all PRs
- .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with build/test/docker checklist
- Issue templates: bug report (YAML form) + feature request + chooser config
- CONTRIBUTING.md: fork/branch/PR workflow, conventional commits, dev setup
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md: Contributor Covenant v2.1
- LICENSE: AGPLv3 full text (enables GitHub license detection)

commit.message:
perf: paralléliser requêtes DB + emails fire-and-forget (8 optimisations backend)

users.service: email invitation fire-and-forget (~500ms économisés sur POST /team)
payments.service: create() → validatePaymentSequence + generateReceiptNumber en parallèle
payments.service: getStats() → 7 requêtes séquentielles → 1 Promise.all concurrent
classes.service: createDefaultClasses → N×M creates séquentiels → Promise.all
classes.service: saveSubjects → upserts séquentiels → Promise.all dans la transaction
grades.service: getStats() → 3 requêtes séquentielles → Promise.all
admin.service: resendDirectorInvite → email fire-and-forget
auth.service: resendVerificationEmail → email fire-and-forget

Hybrid audit: deterministic checks + slimmed LLM

Move 70-80% of checks (color, typography, spacing, radius, components)
into pure-function deterministic engine. LLM only called for semantic/visual
analysis with stripped-down node data. Falls back to deterministic results
if LLM fails. ~60-80% token reduction.

- Add design-system/tokens.json as structured source of truth
- Add api/_lib/ modules: types, deterministic, llm, merge
- Rewrite api/audit.ts as thin orchestrator
- Bundle tokens.json via vercel.json includeFiles

commit.message:
Suite-first evaluation and diverse master prompt

Switch benchmark gating from single-board profile to the full
profile-suite average so optimizations must generalize across
boards. Add 3 generated boards to the suite (6 total). Overhaul
the master prompt to enforce category diversity, detect exhausted
veins, and rotate through search/eval/pruning/TT/data-structure
idea categories instead of tunnel-visioning on move-ordering tweaks.

commit.message:
fix: convert message field names from snake_case to camelCase

Backend was returning messages with snake_case field names (create_time,
from_username, etc.) but frontend expected camelCase (createTime,
fromUsername, etc.), causing 'Invalid time value' error when trying to
create Date objects.

- Add field name conversion in ConversationService.getMessages()
- Update tests to match new data format

Fixes the 'RangeError: Invalid time value' error when loading messages.

commit.message:
Colour/UX overhaul: professional grey palette with industry-standard tokens (#91)

* Realign colour tokens with Shadcn conventions and industry standards

Replace warm ivory/orange/cyan palette with professional white/grey base.
Changes --background to #FFFFFF, --secondary and --accent to neutral grey
(#F1F5F9), --muted to cool grey (#F6F8FA). Migrates 4 components that
intentionally use orange to direct --mcr-orange brand colour references.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refine colour overhaul: Facebook-matched values + dialog contrast fix

Background #F6F8FA → #F2F4F7 (Facebook's --web-wash) for stronger
card contrast. Muted #EFF2F5 → #F0F2F5 (Facebook's --comment-background).
Dialog/AlertDialog/Sheet backgrounds changed from bg-background to
bg-card so form inputs are visible inside modals (matching Facebook's
white dialog/popover approach). Updated CLAUDE.md lessons learned,
plan docs, and hr-plan key file references.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor accent tokens to reference secondary via var()

DRY improvement per Gemini review: --accent and --secondary are
intentionally identical (Shadcn convention). Using var(--secondary)
ensures a single point of change if the value is updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial release: Python Loops interactive learning guide

10-page progressive web learning experience teaching while/for loops
to SCQF Level 6 students. Pure HTML/CSS/vanilla JS, no dependencies.

- Pages 1-3: Foundations (why loops, while anatomy, infinite loops)
- Pages 4-7: Core concepts (for loop, range(), lists, break/continue)
- Pages 8-10: Synthesis (loop choice, patterns, robot maze game)

Shared JS components: step-through animator, syntax highlighter,
nav bar, drag-drop reorder, robot maze game engine.

- Forma spojena na /api/contact fetch endpoint
- Dodani CSS stilovi za success/error status poruke
- Ažurirani logo, broj mobitela i footer godina na svim stranicama

fix: push tags separately to ensure they reach remote

--follow-tags only pushes annotated tags, but lightweight tags
created by git tag -f were silently skipped.

commit.message:
fix: Mermaid — пастельные цвета в источниках диаграмм (56 файлов)

Правильный подход: цвета задаются в исходниках, а не через CSS/JS хаки.
- 56 файлов: тёмные fill (#005500, #0055BB, #885500, #CC4400...) → пастельные
- color:#fff → color:#1a1a1a (тёмный текст на светлом фоне)
- Убраны fill-opacity, JS пост-рендер, все !important оверрайды
- fontFamily: ui-sans-serif (система рассчитывает ноды под тот же шрифт)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: 1c-erp-quartz
repository.description: 1C:ERP в Q-Commerce — образовательный курс

commit.message:
fix(calendar): Kündigungsfenster 30 Tage vor Deadline statt am selben Tag

- CANCEL_WINDOW_OPEN war identisch berechnet wie LAST_CANCEL_DAY (beide: expiry - noticePeriod)
- Jetzt: Fenster öffnet 30 Tage VOR Deadline, LAST_CANCEL_DAY bleibt Deadline
- CANCEL_REMINDER entfernt (redundant, da Fenster jetzt selbst 30d vorher liegt)
- Logische Reihenfolge: Fenster → 7-Tage-Warnung → Letzter Tag → Ablauf

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
